title: ConvertKit vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Omnisend. Learn which email marketing platform suits creators, bloggers, and e-commerce businesses best.
h1: ConvertKit vs Omnisend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: ConvertKit and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators and course sellers, while Omnisend specializes in e-commerce with integrated SMS and push notifications. This comparison will help you decide which platform aligns best with your business needs and budget.
choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller looking for specialized tools like landing pages and commerce features
You need a more generous free tier with up to 1,000 subscribers before paying
You prefer a platform with strong creator-focused community and resources
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need email and SMS marketing combined
You want the lowest starting price ($16/month vs $29/month)
You need advanced segmentation and pre-built workflow automation for product-based businesses

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. ConvertKit excels for creators and content businesses, while Omnisend is superior for e-commerce brands. ConvertKit has higher customer satisfaction on Trustpilot (3.2 vs 4.6), though Omnisend rates higher on G2 (4.5 vs 4.4). Your choice should depend on whether you prioritize creator tools or omnichannel e-commerce capabilities.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend is cheaper overall, starting at $16/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. However, ConvertKit offers a larger free tier (1,000 vs 250 subscribers), which may delay paid upgrades for smaller creators. For long-term costs, Omnisend remains the more budget-friendly option, especially when factoring in that SMS is built-in rather than an add-on.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms typically support subscriber list imports via CSV files or API connections. However, you'll lose SMS-specific data and workflows when migrating from Omnisend to ConvertKit, since ConvertKit doesn't offer SMS functionality. Plan your migration carefully to preserve important subscriber segments and automation history.
